name: math-explorer
description: Explores an optimization-constant problem: reads the canonical constants/<id>.md ledger, fetches and digests the papers behind the current record upper/lower bounds, samples the existing approaches to see what was tried and what the last round's expansion outcome was, and reports the lay of the land — where the gap is exploitable, which bound is softer, what worked and what dead-ended, and relevant papers or analogous angles. Call at the start of each round before any attempt. Pure scouting: does NOT attempt the improvement and does NOT rank.

name: proof-outliner
description: Assembles the round's candidate field. Samples the existing population (up to ~5), and surveys several attack lines (analytic argument, explicit construction, computational certificate) — expanding live approaches and/or opening new angles — with the hard step named in each. Hands the field, each slugged, to the outline-reviewer, which ranks and narrows it. Does NOT rank and does NOT register; it proposes the candidates, not a single finished plan. Call after the explorer, once per problem per round.

name: outline-reviewer
description: The selection layer. Reviews the outliner's field of candidate angles BEFORE any compute is spent: kills the unworkable (wrong technique, can't beat the record, circular), registers approved new angles into the population, and ranks the whole field head-to-head — this is where ranking has signal, with several live approaches side by side pre-build. Narrows the field to the few (1-3) worth building in parallel. Holds both the gate (register_approach) and the ranking (update_ranking).

name: proof-builder
description: Turns a chosen approach into a concrete bound improvement: a rigorous analytic argument, an explicit construction, or a computational certificate built and checked with Bash. Produces the candidate improvement and its verification artifact in the problem's research folder. Call after the outline (and its review) are ready.

name: proof-reviewer
description: Adversarially verifies the candidate bound improvements from the parallel builders: re-runs each certificate, independently re-derives the key inequality, confirms the claimed bound actually beats the value in the canonical table, and assigns a verification level (the repo's * minimally-verified convention). Calls record_outcome once per built approach (marking each stale so next round's outline-reviewer folds the result into the ranking). Does NOT rank. Returns an APPROVE / CHANGES REQUESTED / RETHINK verdict per approach. Call after the proof-builders.
